ddls:
CREATE TABLE "endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each endpoint */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method of the endpoint (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) */,
  "path" TEXT /* Path of the endpoint (e.g., /orders/addItem/[order_id]/[item_id]) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the endpoint */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the endpoint was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the endpoint was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint the metric belongs to */,
  "request_count" INTEGER /* Number of requests made to the endpoint */,
  "failure_count" INTEGER /* Number of failed requests */,
  "median_response_time" REAL /* Median response time of the endpoint */,
  "average_response_time" REAL /* Average response time of the endpoint */,
  "min_response_time" REAL /* Minimum response time of the endpoint */,
  "max_response_time" REAL /* Maximum response time of the endpoint */,
  "average_content_size" REAL /* Average content size of the endpoint */,
  "response_time_stddev" REAL /* Standard deviation of response times */,
  "response_time_variance" REAL /* Variance of response times */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_time_percentiles" (
  "percentile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response time percentile */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric the percentile belongs to */,
  "percentile_50" REAL /* 50th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_66" REAL /* 66th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_75" REAL /* 75th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_80" REAL /* 80th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_90" REAL /* 90th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_95" REAL /* 95th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_98" REAL /* 98th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_99" REAL /* 99th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_99_9" REAL /* 99.9th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_99_99" REAL /* 99.99th percentile response time */,
  "percentile_100" REAL /* 100th percentile response time */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the percentile was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("percentile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_time_percentiles_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint the request was made to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the request */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Time the request was made */,
  "response_time" REAL /* Time it took to respond to the request */,
  "request_body" TEXT /* Body of the request */,
  "response_body" TEXT /* Body of the response */,
  "request_headers" TEXT /* Headers of the request */,
  "response_headers" TEXT /* Headers of the response */,
  "query_params" TEXT /* Query parameters of the request */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the request failed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_rates" (
  "rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request rate */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the rate belongs to */,
  "requests_per_second" REAL /* Number of requests per second made by the user */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rate was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_rates_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "failure_rates" (
  "failure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each failure rate */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint the failure rate belongs to */,
  "failures_per_second" REAL /* Number of failures per second for the endpoint */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the failure rate was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("failure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_failure_rates_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content size */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint the content size belongs to */,
  "average_content_size" REAL /* Average content size for the endpoint */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the content size was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_sizes_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_time_distributions" (
  "distribution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response time distribution */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint the distribution belongs to */,
  "min_response_time" REAL /* Minimum response time for the endpoint */,
  "max_response_time" REAL /* Maximum response time for the endpoint */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the distribution was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distribution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_time_distributions_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request that caused the error */,
  "error_code" INTEGER /* Error code */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message */,
  "stack_trace" TEXT /* Stack trace of the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)
